mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index signals expanding manufacturing output and higher corporate revenues, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO value 104.0998 as of t-2mo indicates positive momentum
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when INDPRO increases month-over-month over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.2 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor income and weakening consumer demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.2 percentage points over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when the trigger fires over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts signal broad-based demand acceleration that outpaces supply constraints, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 474948 and HOUST at 1215 in recent prints
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive during joint up-months of RSAFS and HOUST over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, month-over-month increases in the consumer price index signal accelerating inflation that compresses equity valuation multiples through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 244.158 and FEDFUNDS at 0.9 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when CPIAUCSL rises during periods of Fed funds rate increases over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below their trailing twelve-month moving average signals a rollover in residential investment and cyclical demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its trailing 12-month moving average
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of accelerating payroll employment growth and a federal funds rate below 1.5 percent generates excess liquidity in a fully employed economy, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 146063 and FEDFUNDS at 0.9 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when both PAYEMS increases and FEDFUNDS is below 1.5% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A negative three-month annualized change in industrial production signals a contraction in manufacturing activity and corporate earnings pressure, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized change is less than zero
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In tight labor markets, month-over-month increases in retail sales signal overheated consumer demand that elevates inflation risk and anticipated monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when RSAFS rises while UNRATE is below 5.0% over any 3-year rolling window.
